metadata.title: Adverse Events Associated with Recombinant Human Bone Morphogenetic Protein 2 (rhBMP-2) in Spine Surgery: A Comprehensive Literature Review
metadata.abstract: BACKGROUND CONTEXT: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 (rhBMP-2) is a very potent osteogenic growth factor that has been used successfully in various spine fusions, obviating the need for autologous iliac crest bone graft harvest and therefore avoiding the associated morbidities. PURPOSE: In the past few years, a tremendous increase in rhBMP-2 usage was noted, and concerns regarding costs, benefits, and safety issues were raised by many. The goal of this work was to provide a comprehensive review of the adverse events and complications associated with use of rhBMP-2. STUDY DESIGN: Literature review. METHODS: This is a review of the current literature on the reported adverse events, complications, and concerns associated with rhBMP-2 use. RESULTS: This article discusses the wide spectrum of adverse outcomes related to rhBMP-2 use in the lumbar and the cervical spine; retrograde ejaculation, antibodies formation, postoperative radiculitis, postoperative nerve root injury, ectopic bone formation, vertebral osteolysis/edema, dysphagia and neck swelling, hematoma formation, interbody graft lucency, and wound healing complications are reviewed. Cost-related concerns, dosage considerations, carrier types, and theoretical carcinogenesis concerns were also presented. CONCLUSIONS: Despite the excellent spinal fusion rates promoted by this powerful molecule, the increasingly reported adverse outcomes associated with bone morphogenetic protein usage have created real concerns. This article will provide the reader with a good understanding of the reported complications associated with rhBMP-2 use and ultimately help recognize its safety spectrum and limits for better clinical application.

spine_metadata.main_conclusion: Despite excellent spinal fusion rates achieved with rhBMP-2, increasingly reported adverse outcomes including retrograde ejaculation, nerve root injury, ectopic bone formation, vertebral osteolysis, dysphagia, and hematoma have created significant safety concerns that limit its clinical application.

pico.population: Patients undergoing lumbar and cervical spine fusion surgeries
pico.intervention: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 (rhBMP-2) application during spine fusion
pico.outcome: Adverse events, complications, safety profile, fusion rates, cost considerations

effect_measure.label: Excellent fusion rates reported with rhBMP-2
Retrograde ejaculation reported with rhBMP-2 use
Postoperative radiculitis associated with rhBMP-2
Postoperative nerve root injury associated with rhBMP-2
Ectopic bone formation associated with rhBMP-2
Vertebral osteolysis and edema associated with rhBMP-2
Dysphagia and neck swelling associated with rhBMP-2 in cervical fusion
Hematoma formation associated with rhBMP-2
Interbody graft lucency associated with rhBMP-2
Wound healing complications associated with rhBMP-2
Antibody formation against rhBMP-2

chunks.content: BACKGROUND CONTEXT: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 (rhBMP-2) is a very potent osteogenic growth factor that has been used successfully in various spine fusions, obviating the need for autologous iliac crest bone graft harvest and therefore avoiding the associated morbidities.
chunks.summary: rhBMP-2 is a potent osteogenic growth factor used in spine fusion to eliminate the need for autologous bone graft harvest and its associated complications.

chunks.content: PURPOSE: In the past few years, a tremendous increase in rhBMP-2 usage was noted, and concerns regarding costs, benefits, and safety issues were raised by many. The goal of this work was to provide a comprehensive review of the adverse events and complications associated with use of rhBMP-2.
chunks.summary: Dramatic increase in rhBMP-2 usage has raised concerns about cost, efficacy, and safety, necessitating a comprehensive adverse event review.

chunks.content: STUDY DESIGN: Literature review. METHODS: This is a review of the current literature on the reported adverse events, complications, and concerns associated with rhBMP-2 use.
chunks.summary: Systematic review of published literature examining all reported adverse events and complications related to rhBMP-2 use in spine surgery.

chunks.content: Retrograde ejaculation is a reported adverse event associated with anterior lumbar interbody fusion (ALIF) procedures utilizing rhBMP-2.
chunks.summary: Retrograde ejaculation identified as a significant adverse event in ALIF procedures using rhBMP-2.

effect_measure.label: Retrograde ejaculation associated with rhBMP-2 use in ALIF

chunks.content: Antibody formation against rhBMP-2 has been reported as a potential immunological concern, which may limit the effectiveness of repeat applications or future use of this biologic agent.
chunks.summary: Immunological response with antibody formation against rhBMP-2 may compromise efficacy with repeated or future applications.

effect_measure.label: Antibody formation against rhBMP-2 documented

chunks.content: Postoperative radiculitis following rhBMP-2 application has been reported as a complication, with symptoms varying in severity and duration.
chunks.summary: Postoperative radiculitis is a documented complication of rhBMP-2 use with variable severity.

effect_measure.label: Postoperative radiculitis associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Postoperative nerve root injury has been associated with rhBMP-2 use, potentially resulting from compression by the biologic material, inflammatory response, or edema surrounding the surgical site.
chunks.summary: rhBMP-2-related nerve root injury may result from material compression, inflammation, or adjacent edema.

effect_measure.label: Postoperative nerve root injury associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Ectopic bone formation in surrounding soft tissues is a well-documented complication of rhBMP-2 use, which may contribute to heterotopic ossification and related morbidity.
chunks.summary: Ectopic bone formation and heterotopic ossification represent significant complications of rhBMP-2 application.

effect_measure.label: Ectopic bone formation associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Vertebral osteolysis and edema have been reported following rhBMP-2 use, characterized by resorption of bone around the interbody graft and associated swelling of vertebral bodies.
chunks.summary: Vertebral osteolysis and edema represent radiologic complications associated with rhBMP-2 application.

effect_measure.label: Vertebral osteolysis and edema associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Dysphagia and neck swelling have been reported as complications specific to cervical spine fusion procedures using rhBMP-2, resulting from local soft tissue inflammation and edema.
chunks.summary: Dysphagia and cervical swelling are documented complications in rhBMP-2-augmented cervical fusion.

effect_measure.label: Dysphagia and neck swelling in cervical rhBMP-2 procedures

chunks.content: Hematoma formation has been reported as a complication associated with rhBMP-2 use, with varying severity from minor self-limited collections to expansile hematomas requiring intervention.
chunks.summary: Hematoma formation ranges from minor self-limited to expansile collections requiring intervention in rhBMP-2 cases.

effect_measure.label: Hematoma formation associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Interbody graft lucency has been observed on imaging in patients treated with rhBMP-2, which may represent granulation tissue, fluid, or incomplete fusion around the graft material.
chunks.summary: Interbody graft lucency on imaging may indicate incomplete fusion or tissue response around rhBMP-2-treated grafts.

effect_measure.label: Interbody graft lucency associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Wound healing complications including infection, dehiscence, and seroma formation have been reported in association with rhBMP-2 use, potentially related to local inflammatory response.
chunks.summary: Wound healing complications including infection, dehiscence, and seroma are associated with rhBMP-2-induced inflammatory response.

effect_measure.label: Wound healing complications associated with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: Cost-related concerns regarding rhBMP-2 have been raised, as the biologic is significantly more expensive than traditional autologous bone graft, creating potential cost-benefit analysis challenges in clinical decision-making.
chunks.summary: Significant cost of rhBMP-2 compared to autologous graft raises cost-benefit concerns in clinical practice.

effect_measure.label: Cost-benefit concerns with rhBMP-2 use

chunks.content: Dosage considerations have been identified as important factors influencing both efficacy and adverse event risk with rhBMP-2, with higher doses potentially increasing complications while lower doses may compromise fusion rates.
chunks.summary: rhBMP-2 dosage represents a critical balance between efficacy and adverse event risk.

effect_measure.label: Dosage considerations critical for rhBMP-2 safety and efficacy

chunks.content: Carrier type for rhBMP-2 delivery has been identified as an important variable, with different carrier materials (collagen, ceramic, synthetic) potentially affecting both osteogenic activity and local inflammatory responses.
chunks.summary: Choice of rhBMP-2 carrier material influences both efficacy and inflammatory response profiles.

effect_measure.label: Carrier type influences rhBMP-2 safety and efficacy

chunks.content: Theoretical carcinogenesis concerns have been raised regarding long-term rhBMP-2 use due to its potent growth-promoting properties, although direct clinical evidence of malignancy remains limited in the spine literature.
chunks.summary: Theoretical carcinogenic risk from rhBMP-2's growth-promoting effects remains a concern despite limited clinical evidence.

effect_measure.label: Theoretical carcinogenesis risk with rhBMP-2

chunks.content: CONCLUSIONS: Despite the excellent spinal fusion rates promoted by this powerful molecule, the increasingly reported adverse outcomes associated with bone morphogenetic protein usage have created real concerns. This article will provide the reader with a good understanding of the reported complications associated with rhBMP-2 use and ultimately help recognize its safety spectrum and limits for better clinical application.
chunks.summary: rhBMP-2 achieves excellent fusion rates but mounting adverse event reports warrant careful consideration of safety profile and appropriate clinical application limits.

effect_measure.label: Excellent fusion rates offset by mounting safety concerns
